entries.label: Morning fog
entries.text: The first ferry often appears before the shoreline is fully visible.

entries.label: Active Pass
entries.text: The waterway changes character with every tide and every wind.

entries.label: Historic Miners Bay
entries.text: The neighbourhood makes more sense on foot than through a windshield.

entries.label: Government Dock
entries.text: Just before sunset, the dock becomes a place to linger rather than arrive.

entries.label: Autumn crocus
entries.text: Small flowers appear among old oyster shells when summer has clearly ended.

entries.label: After rain
entries.text: The forest road grows darker, quieter and more reflective after a storm.

entries.label: Village morning
entries.text: Coffee, groceries and conversation all happen within a few minutes of one another.

entries.label: Evening water
entries.text: The last light often stays on Active Pass after the village has gone quiet.
